// The Collective
"As misunderstanding sparked a reaction, LOWERCASE began to notice the juxtaposition between raccoons and the members of his community."

Raccoons are intelligent mammals who have been ostracized by a society that labelled them as rodents. This misconception maximized their survival instinct, forcing them to fall into the typecasting they'd been placed inside. WRNG CRWD was created to symbolize and serve as a beacon for those who do not fit into the caricature of how Black people are perceived.

Blending both experiences, LOWERCASE and SELF—along with the WRNG CRWD community—have showcased themselves and other talented Black artists through the Month's End shows they curate: a hub where attendees network and share their talents across music, photography, film, design, and acting.

// Live & Events
Month's End

Month's End is a recurring event series curated by WRNG CRWD—a space where Black artists, photographers, cinematographers, designers, actors, and musicians come together to network, perform, and build community.

Each show is more than a concert. It's an ecosystem for like-minded creatives who've been told they don't belong—proving the wrong crowd was right all along.

01The GarrisonToronto
02Drake HotelToronto
03Baby GToronto
04East RoomToronto
05SOB'sNew York City
06Sofar HQNew York City
07Grand Canyon TheatreToronto
08TMU CampusToronto
09Dundas SquareToronto

© 2026 WRNG CRWD — Toronto
01 — MUSIC · PRODUCTION
LOWERCASE
Rapper · Producer · Scarborough, Canada

Biography

Canadian-American artist and producer LOWERCASE is establishing himself as an important talent and community fixture. Growing up with frequent trips to learn his roots in Memphis and Jamaica, he developed an eclectic ear for different sounds.

Hailing from Scarborough, his early influences stem from Jay-Z, Memphis artists Gangsta Blacc, Three 6 Mafia, and 8 Ball & MJG, as well as Jamaican artists Bob Marley and Beres Hammond.

From dissecting The Black Album on his mother's iPod Nano to releasing the Lowercase LP, EP MTSB under alias jJ, and his most recent LP Pilot Episode (2022), LOWERCASE continues to expand his artistry. He is the originator of the WRNG CRWD concept and founder of the Month's End show series.

02 — MUSIC
SELF
Rapper · Producer · Toronto, Canada

Biography

SELF is an urban artist from Toronto, Canada, and a key member of the Wrong Crowd collective. As a conduit for the emotions and experiences of others, he channels the tribulations of life through his music, blending influences of hip-hop, R&B, jazz, neo-soul, lo-fi, soft rock, and house music.

With a versatile mix of styles and cadences, SELF expresses his perspectives through soothing, somber tones, evoking deep emotion through both composition and lyrical selection.

From his debut single Moonlight to his early project Roads (EP), and his collaborations with fellow Wrong Crowd members LOWERCASE and Be, SELF consistently showcases his passion for the duality of art. With a primary focus on sample-based production, SELF has cultivated a distinctive sound that is easily identifiable as his own.
